Dollar Tree CEO sends blunt message on prices, economy
content: Shopping for groceries and other essentials used to be a fairly boring experience. You'd make a list, grab your haul, and move on with your afternoon. These days, consumers need to spend more time shopping for groceries and essentials. The reason? Everything is up. In February, food costs rose 3.1% on an annual basis, according to the Consumer Price Index. The cost of groceries specifically rose 2.4%, while the cost of dining out increased 3.9%. Of course, price increases like these tend to hurt lower-income households the most. But even higher earners are feeling squeezed. In a CBS News poll conducted last year, 19% of people with incomes of $100,000 or more rated their financial situation as "bad." And if prices continue to climb, it won't be surprising to see higher earners start to make serious cuts. Dollar Tree acknowledges that everyone is struggling It's not surprising that low and moderate earners are having a hard time keeping up with their bills. But even people with more generous wages are feeling the pressure. A good 92% of consumers cut back on spending in 2025, and that extends to essentials like groceries, according to Resume Now's 2026 Cost-of-Living Crunch Report . Dollar Tree CEO Mike Creedon has seen the shift, too. "It doesn't matter how much money you make, everybody is hurting right now," Creedon said during a conference call last year. Related: Costco cuts prices on eggs, butter, other staples He echoed that sentiment during Dollar Tree's Q3 2025 earnings call . "While the consumer landscape remains uneven, the underlying story remains consistent: all consumers are seeking value." "Lower-income households are depending on us more than ever," Creedon added. But while that may be the case, Dollar Tree has also noted that a growing number of higher earners are frequenting its stores. And there's a reason for that. Dollar Tree has noted that, regardless of income level, all consumers are seeking value amid the current economy. Shutterstock Dollar Tree's new pricing strategy reflects a broad economic shift Dollar Tree said during its most recent earnings call that it had 3 million more households shop at its stores during the quarter compared to the same time last year. But about 60% of those new shoppers came from households earning more than $100,000. For this reason, Dollar Tree had embraced a new multi-price strategy. Rather than limiting its offering to the $1.25 to $1.50 price point, Dollar Tree is aiming for a broader assortment...

content: 1: 以下、ニュー速クオリティでお送りします 2023/05/09(火) 13:46:03.39 ID:jGHUfo8B0 10歳で人生が決まる ドイツの「選別」は、小学校卒業のタイミングではじまる。 小学校は4年制で、その後の進路は三つのコースに分かれる。基幹学校、実科学校、ギムナジウムだ。 ・基幹学校...例えるなら、中卒で就職、もしくは定時制高校に通いながら働くコース。 ・実科学校...例えるなら、工業高校や商業高校から専門学校進学コース。 ・ギムナジウム...卒業後、大学に入学することができる。例えるなら、中高一貫校から大学進学コース。 大学に入学するためにはギムナジウムの卒業が必要なので、例えば、基幹学校や実科学校の卒業生は、大学に入学する資格はない。 つまり、ドイツでは小学校卒業後にどのコースを選ぶかによって、その後受けられる教育や就ける職業が決定されるのである。 引用元: ・https://nova.5ch.net/test/read.cgi/livegalileo/1683607563/ 続きを読む

Walmart is selling a rocking lounge chair and ottoman patio set for $40 with a foldable and portable design
content: TheStreet aims to feature only the best products and services. If you buy something via one of our links, we may earn a commission. Why we love this deal The transition from winter to spring isn't always easy. There's that period when there are hints of spring with warmer and sunnier days mixed in with a mix of clouds, rain, and sometimes even snow that make it feel like we're back to the middle of winter. Just this week, there was a glimmer of hope with a few days in the high 60s that made me want to step outside and soak up as much sun as I could. With snow still on the ground and my larger main patio set covered with the cushions hidden away in an outdoor storage box , I wished I had a small and portable patio set that I could break out on a whim. I came across the ​​ Costway 2-Piece Sling Lounge Rocking Chair and Ottoman Patio Set at Walmart , and it looks like the perfect spring-ready patio upgrade that would make lounging outside after hibernating all winter a breeze. Right now, it's on sale for $40 with 42% off its regular price of $60. ​​Costway 2-Piece Sling Lounge Rocking Chair and Ottoman Patio Set, $40 (was $69) at Walmart C Why do shoppers love it? While some patio sets are modular and movable, not all are made to be portable. This folding patio set has the potential to bring on the relaxation anywhere you go. The folding lounge chair has a sling design with armrests and a curve that gives you a comfortable, ergonomic seat. Did we also mention it's a rocking chair? On top of the reclined backrest, you can rock gently and enjoy the spring weather. But it doesn't stop there. The set is completed with a matching ottoman that you can kick your feet up on. The patio set is made of comfortable and durable materials that can hold up against spring and summer weather. The backrest, seat, and ottoman top are all made of PE rattan. Not only is it breathable (which will come in handy once it gets even warmer out), but it's also resistant to fading. For durability, the frame is made of metal that's resistant to rusting. When it comes to the weight capacity, the chair can hold up to 350 pounds and the ottoman can hold up to 110 pounds. The relaxation-ready design is a clear win, but I really love that the patio set is foldable and portable. As I mentioned before, the transition into spring can bring on some warm and some cold days. During the warm and sunny moments, this set would be easy to set up in the backyard to lounge for a bit. Then, when you're...

title: Scott Mesler marks 25 years at Cutco
content: OLEAN — Scott Mesler, transfer polish machine operator for Cutco Cutlery Corp., is celebrating 25 years of service with the company this week. Mesler joined Cutco on March 12, 2001, as a mold press operator. He has also held the position of group leader, and assumed his current position on Nov. 30, 2022. A veteran of the U.S. Army, Mesler lives in Eldred, Pa., with his wife, Jackie. They have two daughters, Kendra and Montanna.

Chris Chase marks 25 years at Cutco
content: OLEAN — Chris Chase, distribution center operator for Cutco Cutlery Corp., is celebrating 25 years of service with the company this week. Chase joined Cutco on March 12, 2001, as an etcher. She was also induction heater operator, assembler, kitchen tool operator and utility finisher, before assuming her current position on Feb. 25, 2019. Chase lives in Allegany with her husband, David. She has one daughter, Amanda.

BCA: Nonpublic criminal history info on nearly 600 people was online
content: The Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension notified the public Wednesday that some records for 595 people were accessible on the state’s public criminal history website when they should not have been. The records were available “for a varying amount of time,” and it took about one month to complete the analysis and testing to fix the issue, which happened Feb. 25. The majority of the records affected were criminal charges that had resulted in a conviction but were later dismissed or vacated, according to Jill Oliveira, a BCA spokeswoman. “Criminal history records held by the BCA are kept in the Minnesota Criminal History System,” the BCA said in a statement. “A computer process copies public records from CHS to the public criminal history website. In some instances, CHS was not recognizing the most recent activity on a record, which may have included court actions that would render a record not public. This caused some records to be accessible on the public website in error.” Companies that requested a bulk download of Minnesota criminal history data received the records in response to data requests, and the BCA has begun sending them updated records, Oliveira said. The BCA does not have contact information for people with criminal history records, which is why the agency sent the notice to media around the state and posted information on the BCA’s website , according to Oliveira. “The BCA takes the security of data in all our systems very seriously and regrets that this occurred,” said the statement. The BCA will prepare a report, and people who want to receive a copy can email BCA.DataResponse@state.mn.us to request it by email or mail. title: Bonnies hope third time is the charm vs. George Mason in A-10 tournament
content: OLEAN — The 13th seeded Bonnies blew out La Salle on Wednesday, setting up a date with 5th seeded George Mason in the second round of the A-10 tournament on Thursday. They’ve already fallen to the Patriots twice this year, including once (comfortably) less than two weeks ago. But the Bonnies have come into the postseason with the mentality that the tournament provides a fresh start. “It’s a clean slate,” Bonnies’ coach Mark Schmidt said before Wednesday’s opener. “Everybody’s 0-0. Now let’s go out and try to win until the end.” Lineups: George Mason: Point Guard: 6’1 junior Kory Mincy (14.3 points per game, 39.9% from three) Shooting Guard: 6’5 senior Jahari Long (11.6 points, 4 assists) Small Forward: 6’6 senior Masai Troutman (8.8 points, 3.4 rebounds) Power Forward: 6’8 senior Nick Ellington (4.7 points, 3 rebounds) Center: 6’11 junior Riley Allenspach (13.8 points, 6 rebounds) Key bench players: Emmanuel Kanga, Dola Adebayo St. Bonaventure: Point Guard: 6’2 junior Dasonte Bowen (10.7 points, 5 assists) Shooting Guard: 5’10 junior Buddy Simmons II (16.5 points, 42.3% from three) Small Forward: 6’3 senior Cayden Charles (13.2 points, 6.7 rebounds) Power Forward: 6’7 senior Daniel Egwubine (8.2 points, 40.4% from three) Center: 6’8 senior Frank Mitchell (16.4 points, 10.4 rebounds) Key bench players: Andrew Osasuyi, Joe Grahovac Facing George Mason: For most of the season, the Bonnies have had bright spots offensively, but struggled due to an inability to get stops. Against George Mason, breaking down their defense has been the problem. In their first game, the Bonnies were firing on all cylinders offensively for nearly 3⁄4 of the game. But they blew a 14 point lead late, finishing with just one field goal made in the last 11 minutes. In their second matchup, the Bonnies never really got going on that end, finishing with a season low of 58 points. While other teams in the conference have sent double teams at Mitchell in the post consistently, the Patriots have often guarded him straight-up, with help from defensive rotations. They have the luxury of using the 6’11, 250 pound Allenspach to guard him for most of the game, and players like the 6’10 Sivka coming off the bench. Their aggressive perimeter defense has also made things more difficult for the Bonnies’ guards. While Mitchell has been very productive against single coverage in their two matchups, averaging 20 points and 11 rebounds across the two games, it’s been more difficult for other Bonnies,...

content: 1: 以下、ニュー速クオリティでお送りします 2026/03/11(水) 19:17:04.45 ID:1bgiHWP80 BE:632443795-BRZ(10000) 【アルヴェルの最強対抗馬?】メルセデス・ベンツ新型「VLE」正式発表...“押し出し強め”の顔面で超高級ミニバン戦争が始まるか 見た目が強すぎる...新型「VLE」は“顔面圧”で勝負 メルセデス・ベンツは3月11日、新型BEVバン「VLE」を発表した。 VLEは、新開発のモジュラー/フレキシブル/スケーラブルな「バン・アーキテクチャ」を採用する第1号車で、同社はこれを“空間の概念を一新したグランド・リムジン”と位置づける。 最大8人乗りの室内空間に、デジタル体験を強化するMB.OS(メルセデス・ベンツ・オペレーティングシステム)を組み合わせ、移動そのものを“くつろぎの体験”に近づけるとしている。 左右に電動スライドドアを備え、要望に応じてハンズフリーアクセスにも対応。独立して開閉できるリアウインドウも用意され、狭い場所での荷物の出し入れを想定した構成だ。 走りの面では、新たに車高調整機能付きエアサスペンション「エアマチック」と後輪操舵を採用。車高は上下40mmの調整に対応し、後輪操舵は最大7度とされる。 最小回転直径は10.9m(縁石間、暫定値)とされ、狭い路地や立体駐車場での扱いやすさも強調している。 バッテリーは容量115kWh(暫定値)とされ、航続距離はWLTPで700km超(暫定値)。DC急速充電は最大300kWに対応し、15分で最大355km分(WLTP換算)を充電可能とする。 https://carview.yahoo.co.jp/article/detail/b30fb78cb2caae2e9b9888622d4da172730b2c04/ 引用元: ・https://hayabusa9.5ch.net/test/read.cgi/news/1773224224/ 続きを読む

Olean Class of ’26 on track for 91% grad rate
content: OLEAN — The Olean City School District projects that nearly 91% of students from the Class of 2026 are on track to walk across the auditorium stage and receive their diplomas come June. This data was shared by High School Principal Jeff Andreano at the regular Board of Education meeting Tuesday evening, during which he gave his semi-annual update on the school’s graduation rates and a snapshot of the senior class. Andreano said the current projections for the graduating class show 24 receiving a local diploma, 55 receiving a Regents diploma and 44 receiving an advanced Regents diploma. Additionally, at least five are expected to receive a Career Development and Occupational Studies (CDOS) certificate from CA BOCES, but there may be more. “There should be a lot of smiles in the room because those are really, really good numbers for us,” he said. Additionally, two students are expected to continue in the 2026-27 school year and graduate then, Andreano said. Of this year’s cohort that started with 141 students in ninth grade, 11 of them have dropped out. Andreano said the single biggest factor for why some students don’t graduate is poor attendance. Students are no longer denied credit for their courses after missing so many days, but that many absences would make it hard to pass everything. Meanwhile, students could also be recommended for placement in advanced classes in math, English and social studies. Andreano said the departments and administrators begin talking about which students could qualify for advanced classes around this time of year. “Once they get to our JCC dual-enrollment classes and St. Bonaventure dual-enrollment classes, they have those strong numbers that you need to beat,” Andreano said. “They have a very definite guidelines to get into their classes.” About 77 students will be graduating with college credits. Looking at some of the senior class’s other statistics, Andreano said 46 students attended BOCES programs, which he noted was a high number for Olean. Additionally, 66 participated in athletics, 39 are members of National Honor Society and two have already received full scholarships to an institute of higher learning.

Wellsville village to designate March 25 Jim Raptis Day
content: WELLSVILLE — March 25 will be Jim Raptis Day in the village, honoring the businessman, politician and local advocate who passed away Feb. 22. Mayor Ed Fahs said during the village's meeting on Monday evening that he would sign a proclamation regarding the designation. Coincidentally, March 25 is Greek Independence Day. Raptis, a native of Wellsville, was best known as one of the owners of Texas Hot, a local restaurant he inherited from his father, a Greek immigrant. Seven members of the Raptis family were at the meeting, including his wife, Demmye, and his son, Michael. Demmye Raptis said, “Jim would be very delighted. He loved Wellsville. Thank you for honoring him.” Mike Raptis said, “I think this honor is just right." The proclamation will be signed at the next board meeting on March 23. Prior to the meeting, three visitors brought their concerns to the board. A new landlord expressed her concern that as soon as she had purchased a building, she was immediately made responsible for paying the former resident’s utility bills. She said the practice is unfair to those who are purchasing buildings to help relieve the housing shortage in the village and asked the board to help. Also concerning housing, a resident expressed concern with the conditions at 60 Brooklyn Ave., saying drug use is suspected and that the water, sewer and electric has been cut off. She asked who is responsible for keeping people out of abandoned houses, while she also asked if a bank has foreclosed on the building. She was informed that the board will ask the village's code enforcement agency to look into matter. Allegany County Legislator Kevin Laforge, who was attending the meeting on a different matter, said he will look into the foreclosure matter as well. Main Street landlord Marshall Green asked the board to approach the Chamber of Commerce's policy of renting spaces in front of businesses for Main Street activities. He said years ago, businesses didn't have to pay for using the space in front of their buildings and that now they are being charged. “Business owners shouldn't be penalized for using the spaces in front of their buildings during Main Street events,” he said. The board will address the matter with the Chamber. Green also wondered if the board would address the matter of horse feces on the streets left behind from horse-drawn Amish buggies. It was noted that the Amish were informed of the matter a few years ago and complied with the board's request to deal with the...

Amazon's Nike dri-fit long sleeve is on sale for only $27
content: TheStreet aims to feature only the best products and services. If you buy something via one of our links, we may earn a commission. Why we love this deal Gym goers and avid workout enthusiasts know that part of getting a good workout in is making sure you're properly prepared, and a lot of the starts with the right clothes. Wearing the wrong fabrics can cause discomfort, chafing, overheating, and irritation while you run on the treadmill or lift weights, which not only leaves you uncomfortable, but it can cause you to be distracted and get in the way of finishing your workout safely and on a strong note. Opting for the right fabrics and choosing pieces specifically designed for intense activities is key for remaining cool and comfortable no matter how you get your heart rate up. And while it's always worth it to invest in a few key pieces when you know they're worth it and will hold up, when you can get high-quality for an affordable price, there's no reason why you shouldn't jump at the opportunity. Amazon is selling the Nike One Classic Dri-Fit Long-Sleeve Top for as much as 45% off, a better deal than the offer for the very same shirt on the Nike website right now. Originally $50, the workout top is available starting from $27 right now, whereas it's originally $55 through Nike directly and only on sale for $46. And an even better bonus? It's available in three more colors on Amazon. Nike One Classic Dri-Fit Long-Sleeve Top, From $27 (was $50) at Amazon Courtesy of Amazon Why do shoppers love it? Comfort, elasticity, and moisture-wicking properties — three key things that this workout shirt has. Made with a polyester and elastane blend, this shirt is designed to feel super soft, stretch and move easily with the body, and wick away sweat so that you aren't left with a sopping damp shirt in the middle of your workout. Polyester does lack breathability, so if you run hot in the gym or when you workout, you might opt for something a bit more breathable, but its moisture-wicking properties pull sweat away from the skin to the outer surface, so the fabric feels lightweight and dry no matter how intense your workout is getting. The elastane helps the shirt keep its shape, no matter how much it's stretched out, allowing you to bike, run, squat, lunge, lift, or simply walk with ease. Nike uses their own specifically made Dri-FIT technology, which is a polycotton fabric that's all about keeping you comfortable and dry by supporting the body's natural cooling...

St. Paul police investigating Macalester student’s report that professor urinated on backpack
content: St. Paul police are investigating a Macalester College student’s report that a professor urinated on her belongings. The student’s backpack was unattended for several minutes in a classroom building in December and, when she returned, she found urine on it, said Alyssa Arcand, a St. Paul police spokeswoman. An officer wrote a report this week, classifying it as fourth-degree intentional damage to property, after receiving information last month. The investigation is ongoing and police have not made an arrest, Arcand said. A Pioneer Press message sent to the man’s Macalester College email address Wednesday received an auto reply saying he “is no longer at Macalester.” College spokesman Babs Santos said they don’t comment on personnel matters. He also said the college does not have a statement on the matter. Imani Cruzen contributed to this report.
